claim: High-fidelity LLMs (GPT 5.2, Opus 4.5) can write better frontend code than many human engineers, including generating accessibility features without being asked
actionable: Reconsider UI generation workflows - delegate component creation to models and optimize human effort toward design decisions and review
source_title: Beyond Components: Designing Generative UI for MCP Apps

claim: Three-tier UI generation spectrum exists: Static components (predefined React elements), Declarative UI (LLM-generated JSON/YAML mapped to components), and Generative UI (LLM writes HTML/CSS/JS directly)
actionable: Choose UI generation tier based on needs: static for consistency, declarative for balance, generative for flexibility but requires sandboxing
source_title: Beyond Components: Designing Generative UI for MCP Apps

claim: Declarative UI (JSON/YAML-based component mapping) is currently the optimal balance between flexibility and consistency for production agentic systems
actionable: Use JSON Render or similar declarative systems to enable dynamic UI generation while maintaining design system consistency
source_title: Beyond Components: Designing Generative UI for MCP Apps

claim: MCP apps with double iframe sandboxing are the best delivery mechanism for generative UI because they provide authentication, tool calling, message passing, and containment by default
actionable: Use MCP protocol and iframe-based sandboxing when deploying LLM-generated UI to users in production
source_title: Beyond Components: Designing Generative UI for MCP Apps

claim: The future of agentic interfaces will move beyond static components toward human-agent collaborative experiences where agents and humans co-edit and refine shared artifacts
actionable: Design agentic workflows to include collaborative editing surfaces (like Excalidraw) rather than one-way output from agent to user
source_title: Beyond Components: Designing Generative UI for MCP Apps
